meta.title: Header Main
meta.description: Edit-mode planning for 3 user-reported issues: fixed-offset math, hamburger visibility, and padding in closed state

changes.title: Fix mobile menu max-height calculation (--fixed-offset math)
changes.problem: The mobile menu uses `max-h-[calc(100dvh-var(--fixed-offset,80px))]` when open. But `--fixed-offset` only represents the distance from viewport top to the header's top edge (fixed siblings + admin bar + 32px gap). It does NOT account for the header bar's own height. Result: the open menu overflows the viewport by the header's height (~72px).

analysis.whatFixedOffsetIs: Sum of fixed-positioned siblings above header + admin bar + 32px padding. Set in JS calculateFixedOffset() at line 105-119 of HeaderMain.js.
analysis.whatIsMissing: The header bar's own height (header.offsetHeight). The available space for the menu is: viewport - offset-from-top - header-bar-height.
analysis.additionalProblem: --fixed-offset is only set after scroll events. On initial page load before any scrolling, --fixed-offset may not be set, and the fallback 80px is an approximation that may be wrong.

solution.approach: Compute a JS-set CSS variable `--menu-max-height` that correctly calculates available space. Set it on the header element whenever the menu opens and on resize. The CSS then simply uses `var(--menu-max-height)` instead of the calc expression.

jsChanges.description: Add a `updateMenuMaxHeight()` function that computes: `window.innerHeight - header.getBoundingClientRect().top - header.offsetHeight` (clamped to minimum 200px). Call it: (1) inside `openMenu()`, (2) on window resize, (3) on scroll when menu is open. Set it as `--menu-max-height` CSS variable on the header element.
jsChanges.implementation: const updateMenuMaxHeight = () => { const rect = header.getBoundingClientRect(); const available = window.innerHeight - rect.top - header.offsetHeight; header.style.setProperty('--menu-max-height', Math.max(200, Math.floor(available)) + 'px'); }; // Call in openMenu(), resize handler, and scroll handler when menu-open

phpChanges.description: In the <nav> element's class attribute (line 142), replace `menu-open:max-h-[calc(100dvh-var(--fixed-offset,80px))]` with `menu-open:max-h-(--menu-max-height)`

tsxChanges.description: In the <nav> element's className (line ~606), replace `max-md:max-h-[calc(100dvh-var(--fixed-offset,80px))]` with `max-md:max-h-(--menu-max-height)`

decisions.decision: Use JS-computed --menu-max-height instead of CSS calc with --fixed-offset
decisions.evidence: User identified that --fixed-offset only accounts for offset-from-top, not header bar height. JS computation is the only reliable way to get all three values (viewport height, top offset, header height) correctly.

changes.title: Fix hamburger toggle button visibility
changes.problem: The hamburger button is completely invisible at mobile widths. Screenshot at 375px confirms: the dark header bar renders but no hamburger icon is visible. The button class includes `**:h-auto` which targets ALL descendants including SVG elements. The MainMenuOpen.svg has explicit width='28' height='16' attributes, but `h-auto` on an SVG can collapse it to 0 height depending on how the browser resolves auto height when the SVG is inside a flex/block context with no explicit height constraint from the parent.

analysis.buttonClasses: md:hidden cursor-pointer w-7 **:w-full **:h-auto transition-colors text-white focus-visible:...
analysis.svgStructure: SVG width=28 height=16 viewBox='0 0 28 16' with 3 rect elements using fill=currentColor
analysis.rootCause: The `**:h-auto` utility strips the SVG's intrinsic height (from width/height attributes). In a context where the parent button has w-7 (28px) but no explicit height, and the child SVG gets h-auto, the SVG height resolves based on CSS auto sizing rules rather than the SVG's intrinsic 16px. The `**:w-full` forces SVG to 100% of button width (28px), but `**:h-auto` doesn't preserve the aspect ratio. The span wrappers around the SVG also get `h-auto` and `w-full`, creating an additional sizing context issue.
analysis.screenshotEvidence: Screenshot at 375px shows header bar with logo on left but no visible hamburger icon on right side.

solution.approach: Remove the blanket `**:h-auto` and `**:w-full` selectors from the button. Instead, size the SVG directly. The button already has `w-7` (28px). The SVG should fill the button width and maintain its aspect ratio. Use explicit classes on the span/SVG instead of wildcard descendant selectors.

phpChanges.description: On the mobile toggle button (line 127), replace `w-7 **:w-full **:h-auto` with `w-7 h-auto`. On the span wrappers (lines 132-133), add `w-full` to the span class directly. The SVG already has width/height attributes and will size correctly without descendant overrides.
phpChanges.oldButtonClasses: md:hidden cursor-pointer w-7 **:w-full **:h-auto transition-colors text-white focus-visible:outline-dotted focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2
phpChanges.newButtonClasses: md:hidden cursor-pointer w-7 transition-colors text-white focus-visible:outline-dotted focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2
phpChanges.spanChanges: Add `w-full` to each span wrapper. The SVGs use width/height attributes and fill=currentColor, so they'll render at intrinsic size within the span's width constraint.

tsxChanges.description: Same change on the mobile toggle button (line ~573). Remove `**:w-full **:h-auto` from button className. Add `w-full` to span classNames.

decisions.decision: Remove wildcard descendant selectors (**:w-full **:h-auto) from hamburger button and use direct sizing
decisions.evidence: User identified that **:h-auto forces SVG to auto height which collapses to 0. Screenshot confirms hamburger is invisible. Direct sizing lets SVGs use their intrinsic dimensions.

changes.title: Fix constant padding breaking closed menu state
changes.problem: The nav element has `max-md:pt-4 max-md:pb-8` (constant padding on mobile). When the menu is closed, `max-h-0` + `overflow-hidden` clips content but NOT padding. The element remains 48px tall (16px top + 32px bottom padding) even when 'closed'. This creates a visible gap below the header bar.

analysis.currentClasses: The <nav> has: max-md:pt-4 max-md:pb-8 (always applied), max-md:max-h-0 (when closed), max-md:overflow-hidden (when closed)
analysis.cssBehavior: overflow:hidden clips the content box, not the padding box. So max-height:0 + overflow:hidden still renders the padding, making the element visibly tall even when 'closed'.
analysis.previousApproach: These were originally `menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8` but were changed to constant values to prevent a visual jump. The border shift issue that prompted the change needs a different solution.

solution.approach: Revert padding to `menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8` (only applied when open). Add padding to the transition list so it animates smoothly with max-height. This prevents the visual jump that originally motivated the constant padding.

phpChanges.description: In the <nav> element (line 142): (1) Change `max-md:pt-4 max-md:pb-8` to `menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8`. (2) Update the transition to include padding: change `max-md:transition-[max-height,opacity]` to `max-md:transition-[max-height,opacity,padding]`.
phpChanges.oldSnippet: max-md:transition-[max-height,opacity] max-md:duration-500 max-md:ease-in-out max-md:max-h-0 max-md:opacity-0 max-md:pointer-events-none max-md:overflow-hidden max-md:pt-4 max-md:pb-8
phpChanges.newSnippet: max-md:transition-[max-height,opacity,padding] max-md:duration-500 max-md:ease-in-out max-md:max-h-0 max-md:opacity-0 max-md:pointer-events-none max-md:overflow-hidden menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8

tsxChanges.description: In the <nav> className (around line 604-610): (1) Move `max-md:pt-4 max-md:pb-8` from the always-applied string to the isMenuOpen conditional branch as `max-md:pt-4 max-md:pb-8`. (2) Add padding to transition: `max-md:transition-[max-height,opacity,padding]`. In the TSX the padding is in the always-true section, move it to the isMenuOpen section.
tsxChanges.note: In the TSX, the current structure applies padding in the always-true base class. Move the padding classes to the isMenuOpen conditional.

decisions.decision: Revert to menu-open: conditional padding with padding added to transition property list
decisions.evidence: User identified that constant padding breaks closed state (48px visible gap). Animating padding alongside max-height provides smooth transition without the visual jump.

verificationSteps.description: Open mobile menu at 375px width. The menu content should not overflow the viewport. The bottom of the menu (CTA button area) should be visible without scrolling past the viewport edge.
verificationSteps.method: Screenshot at 375px with actions: click hamburger, wait 600ms, verify menu fits within viewport

verificationSteps.description: Scroll down on mobile so header becomes fixed, then open the menu. The max-height should update to account for the new position.
verificationSteps.method: Screenshot at 375px with actions: scroll down 300px, click hamburger, wait 600ms

verificationSteps.description: At 375px width, the hamburger icon (3 horizontal bars) should be clearly visible as white bars on the dark header background, right-aligned.
verificationSteps.method: Screenshot at 375px, inspect top header area for visible hamburger icon

verificationSteps.description: Click hamburger to open menu, verify the close icon (X) is also visible.
verificationSteps.method: Screenshot at 375px with actions: click hamburger, wait 600ms, verify X icon visible

verificationSteps.description: When menu is closed on mobile, there should be NO visible gap below the header bar. The nav element should have 0 effective height.
verificationSteps.method: Screenshot at 375px, verify no gap between header bar and page content below

verificationSteps.description: Open and close the menu. Padding should animate smoothly — no visual jump when opening or closing.

implementationOrder: CHANGE-002 (hamburger visibility — simplest fix, unblocks testing of other changes)
CHANGE-001 (max-height JS computation — most complex, requires JS + CSS coordination)

decisions.decision: Use JS-computed --menu-max-height instead of CSS calc with --fixed-offset
decisions.evidence: User identified that --fixed-offset only accounts for offset-from-top, not header bar height

decisions.decision: Remove wildcard descendant selectors from hamburger button
decisions.evidence: User identified **:h-auto causes SVG height collapse. Screenshot confirms invisibility.

decisions.decision: Revert to conditional padding with transition animation
decisions.evidence: User identified constant padding breaks closed state with 48px gap
